aliasEmails.description: Additional email addresses of this user beyond the primary, if any.

badges.description: The badges that a user has earned over their lifetime.

items.description: Displays a user's accomplishment or milestone

displayName.description: The badge name displayed to users

iconConfig.description: Defines how to render an icon

masked.description: Whether the icon should be masked based on current theme.

name.description: The name of the icon if applicable, e.g. the glyph name for `IconType.GLYPH` icons.

url.description: The URL to an image to be displayed if applicable, e.g. the URL for `iconType.URL` icons.

iconConfig.title: IconConfig

pinned.description: The badge should be shown on the PersonAttribution

items.title: Badge

bannerUrl.description: The URL of the person's banner photo.

bio.description: Short biography or mission statement of the employee.

businessUnit.description: Typically the highest level organizational unit; generally applies to bigger companies with multiple distinct businesses.

busyEvents.description: Intervals of busy time for this person, along with the type of event they're busy with.

start.description: The RFC3339 timestamp formatted start time of this event.

end.description: The RFC3339 timestamp formatted end time of this event.

eventType.description: The nature of the event, for example "out of office".

customFields.description: User customizable fields for additional people information.

displayable.description: Determines whether the client should display this custom field

label.description: A user-facing label for this field.

datasourceProfile.description: List of profiles this user has in different datasources / tools that they use.

datasource.description: The datasource the profile is of.

handle.description: The display name of the entity in the given datasource.

url.description: URL to view the entity's profile.

nativeAppUrl.description: A deep link, if available, into the datasource's native application for the entity's platform (i.e. slack://...).

isUserGenerated.description: For internal use only. True iff the data source profile was manually added by a user from within Glean (aka not from the original data source)

department.description: An organizational unit where everyone has a similar task, e.g. `Engineering`.

departmentCount.description: The number of people in this person's department.

directReportsCount.description: The total number of people who directly report to this person, or 0

endDate.description: If a former employee, the last date of employment.

externalProfileLink.description: Link to a customer's internal profile page. This is set to '#' when no link is desired.

firstName.description: The first name of the person

inviteInfo.description: Information regarding the invite status of a person.

inviteTime.description: The time this person was invited in ISO format (ISO 8601).
inviteTime.x-speakeasy-deprecation-message: Deprecated on 2026-02-05, removal scheduled for 2026-10-15: Use ChannelInviteInfo instead

invites.description: Latest invites received by the user for each channel

reminderTime.description: The time this person was reminded in ISO format (ISO 8601) if a reminder was sent.
reminderTime.x-speakeasy-deprecation-message: Deprecated on 2026-02-05, removal scheduled for 2026-10-15: Use ChannelInviteInfo instead

signUpTime.description: The time this person signed up in ISO format (ISO 8601).

isOrgRoot.description: Whether this person is a "root" node in their organization's hierarchy.

isSignedUp.description: Whether the user has signed into Glean at least once.

lastExtensionUse.description: The last time the user has used the Glean extension in ISO 8601 format.

lastName.description: The last name of the person

location.description: User facing string representing the person's location.

loggingId.description: The logging id of the person used in scrubbed logs, tracking GA metrics.

managementChain.description: The chain of reporting in the company as far up as it goes. The last entry is this person's direct manager.

obfuscatedId.description: An opaque identifier that can be used to request metadata for a Person.

relatedDocuments.description: A list of documents related to this person.

obfuscatedId.description: An opaque identifier that can be used to request metadata for a Person.

relatedDocuments.description: A list of documents related to this person.

orgSizeCount.description: The total recursive size of the people reporting to this person, or 1

peopleDistance.description: List of people and distances to those people from this person. Optionally with metadata.

obfuscatedId.description: An opaque identifier that can be used to request metadata for a Person.

distance.description: Distance to person, refer to PeopleDistance pipeline on interpretation of the value.

permissions.description:
Describes the permissions levels that a user has for permissioned features. When the client sends this, Permissions.read and Permissions.write are the additional permissions granted to a user on top of what they have via their roles.
When the server sends this, Permissions.read and Permissions.write are the complete (merged) set of permissions the user has, and Permissions.roles is just for display purposes.

canAdminSearch.description: TODO--deprecate in favor of the read and write properties. True if the user has access to /adminsearch

canAdminClientApiGlobalTokens.description: TODO--deprecate in favor of the read and write properties. True if the user can administrate client API tokens with global scope

canDlp.description: TODO--deprecate in favor of the read and write properties. True if the user has access to data loss prevention (DLP) features

read.description: Describes the read permission levels that a user has for permissioned features. Key must be PermissionedFeatureOrObject

additionalProperties.description: List of read permissions (for different scopes but same feature)

items.description: Describes the read permission level that a user has for a specific feature

scopeType.description: Describes the scope for a ReadPermission, WritePermission, or GrantPermission object
scopeType.title: ScopeType

items.title: ReadPermission

read.title: ReadPermissions

write.description: Describes the write permissions levels that a user has for permissioned features. Key must be PermissionedFeatureOrObject

additionalProperties.description: List of write permissions (for different scopes but same feature)

items.description: Describes the write permissions levels that a user has for a specific feature

scopeType.description: Describes the scope for a ReadPermission, WritePermission, or GrantPermission object
scopeType.title: ScopeType

create.description: True if user has create permission for this feature and scope

update.description: True if user has update permission for this feature and scope

delete.description: True if user has delete permission for this feature and scope

items.title: WritePermission

write.title: WritePermissions

grant.description: Describes the grant permission levels that a user has for permissioned features. Key must be PermissionedFeatureOrObject

items.description: Describes the grant permission level that a user has for a specific feature

scopeType.description: Describes the scope for a ReadPermission, WritePermission, or GrantPermission object
scopeType.title: ScopeType

items.title: GrantPermission

grant.title: GrantPermissions

role.description: The roleId of the canonical role a user has. The displayName is equal to the roleId.

roles.description: The roleIds of the roles a user has.

permissions.title: Permissions

phone.description: Phone number as a number string.

photoUrl.description: The URL of the person's avatar. Public, glean-authenticated and Base64 encoded data URLs are all valid (but not third-party-authenticated URLs).

preferredName.description: The preferred name of the person, or a nickname.

profileBoolSettings.description: flag settings to indicate user profile settings for certain items

obfuscatedId.description: An opaque identifier that can be used to request metadata for a Person.

relatedDocuments.description: A list of documents related to this person.

startDate.description: The date when the employee started.

startDatePercentile.description: Percentage of the company that started strictly after this person. Between [0,100).

structuredLocation.description: Detailed location with information about country, state, city etc.

countryCode.description: Alpha-2 or Alpha-3 ISO 3166 country code, e.g. US or USA.

structuredLocation.title: StructuredLocation

items.description: Use `id` if you index teams via Glean, and use `name` and `externalLink` if you want to use your own team pages

externalLink.description: Link to a team page on the internet or your company's intranet

relationship.description: The team member's relationship to the team. This defaults to MEMBER if not set.

items.title: PersonTeam

timezone.description: The timezone of the person. E.g. "Pacific Daylight Time".

timezoneOffset.description: The offset of the person's timezone in seconds from UTC.

x-enumDescriptions.FULL_TIME: The person is a current full-time employee of the company.
x-enumDescriptions.CONTRACTOR: The person is a current contractor of the company.
x-enumDescriptions.NON_EMPLOYEE: The person object represents a non-human actor such as a service or admin account.
x-enumDescriptions.FORMER_EMPLOYEE: The person is a previous employee of the company.

uneditedPhotoUrl.description: The original photo URL of the person's avatar before any edits they made are applied

obfuscatedId.description: An opaque identifier that can be used to request metadata for a Person.

relatedDocuments.description: A list of documents related to this person.

associatedEntityId.description: Which entity in the response that this entity relates to. Relevant when there are multiple entities associated with the response (such as merged customers)

documents.description: A truncated list of documents with this relation. TO BE DEPRECATED.
documents.x-speakeasy-deprecation-message: Deprecated on 2026-02-05, removal scheduled for 2026-10-15: Field is deprecated

datasource.description: The datasource associated with the suggestion.

hasCopyPaste.description: Whether the associated query was at least partially copy-pasted.  If subsequent requests are issued after a copy-pasted query is constructed (e.g. with facet modifications), this bit should continue to be set for those requests.

label.description: A user-facing description to display for the suggestion.

missingTerm.description: A query term missing from the original query on which this suggestion is based.

query.description: The query being suggested (e.g. enforcing the missing term from the original query).

ranges.description: The bolded ranges within the query of the QuerySuggestion.

datasourceFilter.description: Filter results to a single datasource name (e.g. gmail, slack). All results are returned if missing.

datasourcesFilter.description: Filter results to one or more datasources (e.g. gmail, slack). All results are returned if missing.

queryOverridesFacetFilters.description: If true, the operators in the query are taken to override any operators in facetFilters in the case of conflict. This is used to correctly set rewrittenFacetFilters and rewrittenQuery.

x-enumDescriptions.EQUALS: The value is equal to the specified value.
x-enumDescriptions.ID_EQUALS: The value is equal to the specified ID.
x-enumDescriptions.LT: The value is less than the specified value.
x-enumDescriptions.GT: The value is greater than the specified value.
x-enumDescriptions.NOT_EQUALS: The value is not equal to the specified value.

x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.EQUALS: The value is equal to the specified value.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.ID_EQUALS: The value is equal to the specified ID.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.LT: The value is less than the specified value.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.GT: The value is greater than the specified value.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.NOT_EQUALS: The value is not equal to the specified value.

isNegated.x-speakeasy-deprecation-message: Deprecated on 2026-02-05, removal scheduled for 2026-10-15: Use relationType instead

values.description: Within a single FacetFilter, the values are to be treated like an OR. For example, fieldName type with values [EQUALS Presentation, EQUALS Spreadsheet] means we want to show a document if it's a Presentation OR a Spreadsheet.

groupName.description: Indicates the value of a facet, if any, that the given facet is grouped under. This is only used for nested facets, for example, fieldName could be owner and groupName would be Spreadsheet if showing all owners for spreadsheets as a nested facet.

facetFilters.description: A list of filters for the query. An AND is assumed between different facetFilters. For example, owner Sumeet and type Spreadsheet shows documents that are by Sumeet AND are Spreadsheets.

x-enumDescriptions.EQUALS: The value is equal to the specified value.
x-enumDescriptions.ID_EQUALS: The value is equal to the specified ID.
x-enumDescriptions.LT: The value is less than the specified value.
x-enumDescriptions.GT: The value is greater than the specified value.
x-enumDescriptions.NOT_EQUALS: The value is not equal to the specified value.

isNegated.x-speakeasy-deprecation-message: Deprecated on 2026-02-05, removal scheduled for 2026-10-15: Use relationType instead

values.description: Within a single FacetFilter, the values are to be treated like an OR. For example, fieldName type with values [EQUALS Presentation, EQUALS Spreadsheet] means we want to show a document if it's a Presentation OR a Spreadsheet.

groupName.description: Indicates the value of a facet, if any, that the given facet is grouped under. This is only used for nested facets, for example, fieldName could be owner and groupName would be Spreadsheet if showing all owners for spreadsheets as a nested facet.

items.description: Within a single FacetFilterSet, the filters are treated as AND. For example, owner Sumeet and type Spreadsheet shows documents that are by Sumeet AND are Spreadsheets.
items.title: FacetFilterSet

facetFilterSets.description: A list of facet filter sets that will be OR'ed together. SearchRequestOptions where both facetFilterSets and facetFilters set are considered as bad request. Callers should set only one of these fields.

facet.description: The facet whose buckets should be filtered.

prefix.description: The per-term prefix that facet buckets should be filtered on.

facetBucketSize.description: The maximum number of FacetBuckets to return in each FacetResult.

defaultFacets.description: Facets for which FacetResults should be fetched and that don't apply to a particular datasource. If specified, these values will replace the standard default facets (last_updated_at, from, etc.). The requested facets will be returned alongside datasource-specific facets if searching a single datasource.

authTokens.description: Auth tokens which may be used for non-indexed, federated results (e.g. Gmail).

authUser.description: Used by Google to indicate the index of the logged in user. Useful for generating hyperlinks that support multilogin.

expiration.description: Unix timestamp when this token expires (in seconds since epoch UTC).

fetchAllDatasourceCounts.description: Hints that the QE should return result counts (via the datasource facet result) for all supported datasources, rather than just those specified in the datasource[s]Filter

responseHints.description: Array of hints containing which fields should be populated in the response.

x-enumDescriptions.ALL_RESULT_COUNTS: Return result counts for each result set which has non-zero results, even when the request itself is limited to a subset.
x-enumDescriptions.QUERY_METADATA: Returns result counts for each result set which has non-zero results, as well as other information about the search such as suggested spelling corrections.
x-enumDescriptions.SPELLCHECK_METADATA: Return metadata pertaining to spellcheck results.

x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.ALL_RESULT_COUNTS: Return result counts for each result set which has non-zero results, even when the request itself is limited to a subset.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.QUERY_METADATA: Returns result counts for each result set which has non-zero results, as well as other information about the search such as suggested spelling corrections.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.SPELLCHECK_METADATA: Return metadata pertaining to spellcheck results.

timezoneOffset.description: The offset of the client's timezone in minutes from UTC. e.g. PDT is -420 because it's 7 hours behind UTC.

disableSpellcheck.description: Whether or not to disable spellcheck.

disableQueryAutocorrect.description: Disables automatic adjustment of the input query for spelling corrections or other reasons.

returnLlmContentOverSnippets.description: Enables expanded content to be returned for LLM usage. The size of content per result returned should be modified using maxSnippetSize. Server may return less or more than what is specified in maxSnippetSize. For more details, see https://developers.glean.com/guides/search/llm-content.

inclusions.description: A list of filters which restrict the search results to only the specified content.

containerSpecs.description: Specifications for containers that should be used as part of the restriction (include/exclude). Memberships are recursively defined for a subset of datasources (currently: SharePoint, OneDrive, Google Drive, and Confluence). Please contact the Glean team to enable this for more datasources. Recursive memberships do not apply for Collections.

ugcType.description: The type of the user generated content (UGC datasource).

contentId.description: The numeric id for user generated content. Used for ANNOUNCEMENTS, ANSWERS, COLLECTIONS, SHORTCUTS.

docType.description: The specific type of the user generated content type.

ugcType.description: The type of the user generated content (UGC datasource).

ugcId.description: The string id for user generated content. Used for CHATS.

docType.description: The specific type of the user generated content type.

inclusions.title: RestrictionFilters

exclusions.description: A list of filters specifying content to avoid getting search results from. Exclusions take precendence over inclusions and other query parameters, such as search operators and search facets.

containerSpecs.description: Specifications for containers that should be used as part of the restriction (include/exclude). Memberships are recursively defined for a subset of datasources (currently: SharePoint, OneDrive, Google Drive, and Confluence). Please contact the Glean team to enable this for more datasources. Recursive memberships do not apply for Collections.

docType.description: The specific type of the user generated content type.

ugcId.description: The string id for user generated content. Used for CHATS.

docType.description: The specific type of the user generated content type.

exclusions.title: RestrictionFilters

resultTab.description: The result tab associated with the suggestion.

id.description: The unique ID of the tab. Can be passed in a search request to get results for that tab.

count.description: The number of results in this tab for the current query.

datasource.description: The datasource associated with the tab, if any.

datasourceInstance.description: The datasource instance associated with the tab, if any.

resultTab.title: ResultTab

searchProviderInfo.description: Information about the search provider that generated this suggestion.

searchLinkUrlTemplate.description: URL template that can be used to perform the suggested search by replacing the {query} placeholder with the query suggestion.

searchProviderInfo.title: SearchProviderInfo

relation.description: How this document relates to the including entity.

x-enumDescriptions.CANONICAL: Canonical documents for the entity, such as overview docs, architecture docs elastic.

x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.CANONICAL: Canonical documents for the entity, such as overview docs, architecture docs elastic.

results.description: A truncated list of documents associated with this relation. To be used in favor of `documents` because it contains a trackingToken.

bannerUrl.description: A link to the team's banner photo.

businessUnit.description: Typically the highest level organizational unit; generally applies to bigger companies with multiple distinct businesses.

canBeDeleted.description: can this team be deleted. Some manually ingested teams like GCS_CSV or PUSH_API cannot

createdFrom.description: For teams created from docs, the doc title. Otherwise empty.

customFields.description: Customizable fields for additional team information.

displayable.description: Determines whether the client should display this custom field

label.description: A user-facing label for this field.

datasource.description: the data source of the team, e.g. GDRIVE

datasourceProfiles.description: The datasource profiles of the team

datasource.description: The datasource the profile is of.

handle.description: The display name of the entity in the given datasource.

url.description: URL to view the entity's profile.

nativeAppUrl.description: A deep link, if available, into the datasource's native application for the entity's platform (i.e. slack://...).

isUserGenerated.description: For internal use only. True iff the data source profile was manually added by a user from within Glean (aka not from the original data source)

department.description: An organizational unit where everyone has a similar task, e.g. `Engineering`.

isUserGenerated.description: true iff the email was manually added by a user from within Glean (aka not from the original data source)

externalLink.description: Link to a team page on the internet or your company's intranet

lastUpdatedAt.description: when this team was last updated.

loggingId.description: The logging id of the team used in scrubbed logs, client analytics, and metrics.

memberCount.description: Number of members on this team (recursive; includes all individuals that belong to this team, and all individuals that belong to a subteam within this team)

items.description: Metadata about the relationship of a person to a team.

customRelationshipStr.description: Displayed name for the relationship if relationship is set to `OTHER`.

relationship.description: The team member's relationship to the team. This defaults to MEMBER if not set.

photoUrl.description: A link to the team's photo.

status.description: whether this team is fully processed or there are still unprocessed operations that'll affect it

datasource.description: The datasource the custom entity is from.

customData.description: Custom fields specific to individual datasources

stringListValue.description: list of strings for multi-value properties

customData.title: CustomData

objectType.description: The type of the entity. Interpretation is specific to each datasource

roles.description: A list of user roles for the custom entity explicitly granted by the owner.

id.description: A unique identifier for the group. May be the same as name.

datasourceInstance.description: Datasource instance if the group belongs to one e.g. external groups.

provisioningId.description: identifier for greenlist provisioning, aka sciokey

role.description: A user's role with respect to a specific document.
role.title: UserRole

ugcType.description: The type of the user generated content (UGC datasource).

contentId.description: The numeric id for user generated content. Used for ANNOUNCEMENTS, ANSWERS, COLLECTIONS, SHORTCUTS.

docType.description: The specific type of the user generated content type.

ugcType.description: The type of the user generated content (UGC datasource).

ugcId.description: The string id for user generated content. Used for CHATS.

docType.description: The specific type of the user generated content type.

sourceDocumentSpec.title: DocumentSpec
sourceDocumentSpec.description: The document spec of the object this role originates from. The object this role is included with will usually have the same information as this document spec, but if the role is inherited, then the document spec refers to the parent document that the role came from.

title.description: Title or name of the custom entity.

sourceName.description: The source of this facet (e.g. container_name, type, last_updated_at).

operatorName.description: How to display this facet. Currently supportes 'SelectSingle' and 'SelectMultiple'.

buckets.description: A list of unique buckets that exist within this result set.

count.description: Estimated number of results in this facet.

datasource.description: The datasource the value belongs to. This will be used by the all tab to show types across all datasources.

percentage.description: Estimated percentage of results in this facet.

stringValue.description: The value that should be set in the FacetFilter when applying this filter to a search request.

displayLabel.description: An optional user-friendly label to display in place of the facet value.

iconConfig.description: Defines how to render an icon

masked.description: Whether the icon should be masked based on current theme.

name.description: The name of the icon if applicable, e.g. the glyph name for `IconType.GLYPH` icons.

url.description: The URL to an image to be displayed if applicable, e.g. the URL for `iconType.URL` icons.

iconConfig.title: IconConfig

hasMoreBuckets.description: Returns true if more buckets exist than those returned. Additional buckets can be retrieve by requesting again with a higher facetBucketSize.

groupName.description: For most facets this will be the empty string, meaning the facet is high-level and applies to all documents for the datasource. When non-empty, this is used to group facets together (i.e. group facets for each doctype for a certain datasource)

cursor.description: Pagination cursor. A previously received opaque token representing the position in the overall results at which to start.

totalCount.description: The total number of entities available

hasMoreResults.description: Whether or not more entities can be fetched.

sortOptions.description: Sort options from EntitiesSortOrder supported for this response. Default is empty list.

customFacetNames.description: list of Person attributes that are custom setup by deployment
